task_completion.description: Open the sample scene 'dwanko_run.tnz' in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar, Xsheet, or scene browser clearly shows 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Set up a second camera in the scene without modifying the original camera.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Schematic view shows a second camera node (e.g., 'Camera 2') alongside the original, or a camera selection dropdown in the UI lists multiple cameras.

task_completion.description: Configure the new camera for a close-up shot of the character.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The viewer shows the camera frame (often a red outline) tightly framing the character's head and torso, taking up at least 50% of the frame.
A 'Save Scene As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'multi_camera.tnz' in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/' directory, or the main window title bar updates to 'multi_camera.tnz'.

task_completion.description: Configure the output settings to use the new camera and set the format to PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' window is visible with the 'Camera' dropdown set to the new camera (e.g., Camera 2), the file format set to 'png', and the save path/name configured for '/home/ga/OpenToonz/outputs/closeup.png'.

task_completion.description: Render Frame 1 as a PNG to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A render progress bar, a 'Render completed' message, or a file manager/image viewer showing the resulting 'closeup.png' file containing the close-up shot.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through OpenToonz menus, schematic views, and output settings, rather than using terminal commands to edit the .tnz XML file directly or using ImageMagick to crop the image.

integrity.description: A second camera was genuinely created in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual confirmation of a new camera node in the OpenToonz schematic or camera list, ensuring the agent didn't just overwrite the original camera's parameters.

integrity.description: The final image was rendered from OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The render process is visible within the OpenToonz UI, rather than the agent downloading a pre-cropped image or using external scripts to generate the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Source scene path: '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. Expected save path: '/home/ga/OpenToonz/projects/multi_camera.tnz'. Expected render output: '/home/ga/OpenToonz/outputs/closeup.png'. A valid close-up requires the character's bounding box to occupy at least 50% of the frame (min_bbox_ratio: 0.5). Note: Ensure visual confirmation of a second camera being added in the GUI. Text-based verification scripts may falsely pass the camera count check due to native '<camera' XML tags in the unmodified file, so visual verification of the new camera node/dropdown is critical.
